claim: Enoch appreciates beauty. If Nikolai either works with precision or creates intricate designs (but not both), then he crafts beautiful pieces. Pet does not strive for perfection. Anyone who meets high standards and has a strong work ethic can earn a good reputation. Pet sets high goals. If Enoch either works with precision or creates intricate designs (but not both), then he crafts beautiful pieces. Enoch does not craft beautiful pieces. Enoch pursues hobbies. Enoch either makes artistic pieces or creates intricate designs (or both). Raphael appreciates beauty. For every person, either they pursue hobbies or they can earn a good reputation, but these are not necessarily related. Enoch sets high goals. If Raphael appreciates beauty and has a patient nature, then he is a skilled plasterer. If someone works with precision, then they take pride in their work and meet high standards. If Enoch appreciates beauty and has a patient nature, then he is a skilled plasterer. Raphael does not craft beautiful pieces. For all humans, if someone is a skilled plasterer, then they either create intricate designs or pay attention to detail (or both). Anyone who works diligently either earns a good reputation or builds a client base. If Enoch sets high goals, then he has a strong work ethic and learns quickly.Based on the above information, is the following statement true, false, or uncertain? Enoch pays attention to detail.

supporting.text: If Enoch works with precision, then he takes pride in his work and meets high standards. Since Enoch sets high goals, he likely meets high standards, which could imply he works with precision, and if he works with precision, he pays attention to detail.

attacks.text: If Enoch works with precision, then he takes pride in his work and meets high standards. Since Enoch sets high goals, he likely meets high standards, which could imply he works with precision, and if he works with precision, he pays attention to detail.

The argument A1 is completely irrelevant to the claim about Caroline reducing genetic disorders. It discusses a different person (Enoch) and different concepts (precision, pride, high standards, attention to detail) that have no logical connection to the premises provided about Caroline.

Even if we ignore the irrelevance, A1's internal logic is flawed. 'Setting high goals' does not necessarily mean 'meeting high standards,' and 'meeting high standards' does not necessarily imply 'working with precision.' The reasoning contains unjustified leaps.

supporting.text: Enoch does not craft beautiful pieces. If Enoch either works with precision or creates intricate designs (but not both), then he crafts beautiful pieces. Since he does not craft beautiful pieces, he does not work with precision and does not create intricate designs, so he does not pay attention to detail.

attacks.text: Enoch does not craft beautiful pieces. If Enoch either works with precision or creates intricate designs (but not both), then he crafts beautiful pieces. Since he does not craft beautiful pieces, he does not work with precision and does not create intricate designs, so he does not pay attention to detail.

The argument assumes that not crafting beautiful pieces implies both 'not working with precision' AND 'not creating intricate designs', but the conditional only states that if (precision XOR designs) then beautiful pieces. The negation of the consequent only tells us that (precision XOR designs) is false, which means either both are true or both are false, not necessarily both false.

Even if the logical step is flawed, the conclusion 'he does not pay attention to detail' doesn't follow from the premises. Attention to detail isn't logically tied to precision or intricate designs in the given statements.

The entire argument is irrelevant to the claim about Caroline reducing genetic disorders. The argument discusses Enoch, who isn't mentioned in the claim or background information, so it provides no support for any conclusion about Caroline.

The argument contains a formal logical error: from 'If P then Q' and 'not Q', we can deduce 'not P'. Here P is '(works with precision XOR creates intricate designs)' and Q is 'crafts beautiful pieces'. 'not P' means the XOR is false, which means both components have the same truth value, not necessarily both false.

The argument A7 discusses Enoch, hobbies, and attention to detail, but the claim is specifically about Avah and whether she can solve complex cases. There is no logical connection established between Enoch's hobbies and Avah's capabilities.

The premise 'For every person, either they pursue hobbies or they can earn a good reputation' is presented as a universal rule without justification from the given information. The provided facts do not support this dichotomy, making the foundational logic of A7 highly speculative.

While B2 correctly points out the unsupported universal rule, one could interpret 'pursuing hobbies' and 'earning a good reputation' as not being mutually exhaustive. However, the irrelevance to Avah (point B1) is the more fundamental flaw, rendering the logical validity of the premise secondary.
